message:
fix(fr-command): ACL CAT slow includes all non-fast commands [bd-frankenredis-ifuw partial]

Upstream Redis 7.2 commands.def assigns the SLOW flag implicitly
to every command that lacks the 'fast' flag (any O(N) or O(log N)
command). fr's command_matches_acl_category was checking for an
explicit 'slow' flag in the entry's flags string, which is never
set in fr's COMMAND_TABLE — resulting in 'ACL CAT slow' returning
3 commands vs 271 in upstream.

Mirror upstream's implicit slow=NOT(fast) by inverting the check:
any command without the 'fast' flag is in the slow category.

Subcommand-form names (cmd|sub) and other category gaps tracked
in br-frankenredis-ifuw are still pending.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -11096,7 +11096,11 @@ fn command_matches_acl_category(name: &str, flags: &str, category: &str) -> bool
         "read" => flag_list.contains(&"readonly"),
         "write" => flag_list.contains(&"write"),
         "fast" => flag_list.contains(&"fast"),
-        "slow" => flag_list.contains(&"slow"),
+        // Upstream implicit "slow" category: any command lacking the
+        // 'fast' flag is automatically slow (commands.def assigns
+        // SLOW for everything except a small set of O(1) commands).
+        // (br-frankenredis-ifuw)
+        "slow" => !flag_list.contains(&"fast"),
         "admin" => flag_list.contains(&"admin"),
         "pubsub" => flag_list.contains(&"pubsub"),
         "scripting" => flag_list.contains(&"scripting"),
